Pricing Deep-Dive # Thinkific Pricing: What It Actually Costs Plans from $36 to $149/month (annual) — but the real cost depends on which payment processor you use and which features you need. Updated March 202614 min read [See Ruzuku Pricing](https://www.ruzuku.com/pricing) [Full Thinkific Comparison](https://www.ruzuku.com/compare/thinkific) Thinkific's pricing looks competitive with unlimited courses and students on every plan. But two things catch people off guard: a surcharge of up to 5% per transaction if you use your own Stripe instead of Thinkific Payments, and a 10,000 student cap on all three standard plans. The Basic plan at $36/month (annual) also locks you out of certificates, assignments, payment plans, Zoom integration, and memberships. ## Thinkific's Plans at a Glance All plans with monthly and annual pricing, limits, and key features. | Plan | Monthly | Annual | Tx Fees | Courses | Students | Key Limitation | | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | | Basic | $49/mo | $36/mo | 0% (TCommerce) / 5% (own Stripe) | Unlimited | 10,000 | 5% surcharge if using own Stripe | | StartPopular | $99/mo | $74/mo | 0% (TCommerce) / 2% (own Stripe) | Unlimited | 10,000 | 2% surcharge if using own Stripe | | Grow | $199/mo | $149/mo | 0% (TCommerce) / 1% (own Stripe) | Unlimited | 10,000 | 1% surcharge still on own Stripe | | Plus | Custom | — | Custom | Unlimited | Unlimited | Custom pricing — must contact sales | ### Basic 0% (TCommerce) / 5% (own Stripe) tx fee $49/moor $36/mo annually Courses: UnlimitedStudents: 10,000 - Everything in Basic - Certificates & assignments - Payment plans - 2% surcharge if using own Stripe - 10,000 student cap (same as Basic) ### Grow 0% (TCommerce) / 1% (own Stripe) tx fee $199/moor $149/mo annually Courses: UnlimitedStudents: 10,000 - Everything in Start - Remove Thinkific branding - 3 communities (20 spaces) - 1% surcharge still on own Stripe - 10,000 student cap (same as Basic) ### Plus Custom tx fee Custom Courses: UnlimitedStudents: Unlimited - Unlimited students & products - SCORM-compliant courses - Learning paths with progression - Custom pricing — must contact sales - Branded app only on select Plus plans ## Thinkific Discounts, Coupons & Free Trial What's actually available — and what the coupon sites won't tell you. ### 14-Day Free Trial No credit card required.Thinkific discontinued its free plan and replaced it with a 14-day trial on all paid plans. ### ~25% Annual Billing Discount Annual billing saves 25-27% depending on the plan. Basic drops from $49 to $36/month, Start from $99 to $74/month, and Grow from $199 to $149/month. ### The Truth About Thinkific Coupon Codes Thinkific does not distribute public coupon codes. Results for "Thinkific discount code" or "Thinkific coupon" lead to affiliate sites with fabricated or expired codes. The 14-day free trial and annual billing discount are the only official ways to save. Best deal strategy:Start with the 14-day free trial (no credit card required), then choose annual billing to save up to 27%. The Start plan at $74/month annual is the sweet spot for most creators. Ruzuku offers a permanent free plan with no time limit, unlimited courses, and zero transaction fees on all paid plans — no payment processor surcharges regardless of which Stripe account you use. Nonprofits get 30% off annual plans plus a one-time lifetime license option. ## Every Feature, Every Plan A line-by-line breakdown of what each Thinkific plan includes — and where Ruzuku gives you more for less. | Feature | Basic

Thinkific: 5% on Basic [See Ruzuku pricing](https://www.ruzuku.com/pricing) ## What Thinkific's Pricing Page Doesn't Make Obvious Hidden fees, gotchas, and costs that aren't clear until you're already committed. ### Third-party payment processor surcharge (1-5%) Using your own Stripe instead of TCommerce costs 5% extra on Basic, 2% on Start, 1% on Grow — at $5,000/mo revenue on Basic, that's $250/mo in surcharges ### 10,000 student cap on ALL three standard plans Basic, Start, AND Grow all share the same 10,000 student limit. Unlimited students requires Plus (custom pricing / contact sales) ### Basic plan locks out certificates, assignments, Zoom, and payment plans To get certificates, assignments, payment plans, or Zoom integration, you must upgrade from Basic ($36/mo) to Start ($74/mo annual) ### Branded mobile app is $199/month add-on A branded app on top of the Start plan ($74/mo) brings your total to $273/mo. Only included on select Plus plans ### PayPal payments require Grow ($149/mo annual) If your students want to pay via PayPal, you need the Grow plan — Basic and Start only support TCommerce and credit cards ### SCORM requires Plus (custom pricing) If you need SCORM compliance for corporate training, none of the three standard plans support it — only Plus (contact sales) ## How Does the Thinkific Payments Penalty Work? Thinkific's most misunderstood pricing element is the surcharge for using your own Stripe account instead of Thinkific Payments. If you connect your own Stripe, Thinkific adds a per-transaction surcharge on top of Stripe's standard 2.9% + $0.30: - **Basic ($49/mo):** 5% surcharge - **Start ($99/mo):** 2% surcharge - **Grow ($199/mo):** 1% surcharge On the Basic plan with your own Stripe, you'd pay 2.9% (Stripe) + 5% (Thinkific surcharge) = 7.9% per transaction. At $5,000/month revenue, that's $395 in processing costs — nearly matching Teachable's infamous 7.5% fee. The alternative? Use Thinkific Payments and pay 0% surcharge. But Thinkific Payments locks you into their payment processing ecosystem, which some creators prefer to avoid for accounting, tax, or business reasons. ### Basic Locks You Out of Key Course Features The Basic plan at $36/month (annual) looks like a deal — until you realize it excludes certificates, assignments, payment plans, memberships, Zoom integration, and multimedia content. All of those require the Start plan at $74/month. That's a $38/month jump to get the features most course creators need from day one. ### The Mobile App Add-On Thinkific offers a branded mobile app, but it's a separate add-on at $199/month — potentially doubling your total platform cost. This is a standalone subscription on top of your plan fee. On the Start plan ($99/mo), adding the mobile app brings your total to $298/month. ## What Real Thinkific Users Say ### What Users Appreciate Thinkific's course builder earns a 4.5/5 on G2 across 368 reviews, with consistent praise for ease of use. One G2 reviewer described it as "really easy to use even if you are not a technological boffin." Capterra reviewers highlight the same theme — the platform lets teams launch courses without technical skills. SCORM compliance (on the Grow+ plans) makes Thinkific a legitimate option for corporate training, which most competitors can't match. This is a genuine differentiator for professional development programs. ### Common Complaints > "The payment fee trap is real if you don't use TCommerce. It becomes a silent tax on every sale." Users on [r/OnlineBusiness](https://www.reddit.com/r/OnlineBusiness/) express frustration at being pushed into using Thinkific's payment processor. One Trustpilot reviewer described encountering "hidden transaction fees of 6-9%" despite Thinkific's marketing claiming "0% transaction fees" — because the surcharge for using your own Stripe isn't prominently disclosed. A Trustpilot reviewer in September 2025 reported that Thinkific held their funds for 90 days on a second account — despite having over $1 million in prior sales history on their first account. Payment processing issues like this are particularly damaging for creators who depend on timely payouts. > "Constant limitations of the platform make it very hard to scale." The mobile app add-on also draws criticism. At $199/month on top of plan fees, reviewers note the app has significant feature gaps — students can view content, but key lesson types, community features, and in-app purchasing are missing. ### The Migration Question Thinkific allows student list exports and basic content downloads, but there's no one-click course export. Hosted videos require downloading each individually via links. Active subscriptions cannot be migrated, and transaction reporting only covers Thinkific Payments transactions. If you're using SCORM content, ensure your new platform supports it. Migration typically takes 2-4 weeks. See our [complete Thinkific vs Ruzuku comparison](https://www.ruzuku.com/compare/thinkific), [Thinkific pricing plans breakdown](https://www.ruzuku.com/learn/articles/thinkific-pricing-plans), and [honest Thinkific review](https://www.ruzuku.com/learn/articles/is-thinkific-any-good). You can also view [Thinkific's official pricing page](https://www.thinkific.com/pricing/) to verify current numbers. ## Real-World Cost Scenarios What Thinkific actually costs at three revenue levels — including fees, add-ons, and the plan you'd really need. ### Solo Creator $1,000/mo revenue Thinkific $86/mo Basic (annual): $36/mo Processing fees: $50/mo Annual: $1,032/yr Ruzuku $99/mo Core ($99/mo): $99/mo 0% platform fee (standard Stripe 2.9% + $0.30) Annual: $1,188/yr Thinkific is $13/mo cheaper What you get on Thinkific - Unlimited courses - Custom domain - Quizzes & discussions - Email automation - Abandoned cart recovery What you'll still need - 5% Stripe surcharge = $50/mo on $1K revenue - No certificates or assignments - No payment plans or memberships - No Zoom integration - No student tech support ### Growing Business $5,000/mo revenue Thinkific $174/mo Start (annual): $74/mo Processing fees: $100/mo Annual: $2,088/yr Ruzuku $99/mo Core ($99/mo): $99/mo 0% platform fee (standard Stripe 2.9% + $0.30) Annual: $1,188/yr Save $75/mo ($900/yr) with Ruzuku What you get on Thinkific - Certificates & assignments - Payment plans & memberships - Unlimited Zoom events - Product bundles - CSS/HTML customization What you'll still need - 2% Stripe surcharge = $100/mo on $5K revenue - 10,000 student cap - No branding removal - No PayPal - No API or webhooks - No student tech support ### Established Creator $20,000/mo revenue Thinkific $349/mo Grow (annual): $149/mo Processing fees: $200/mo Annual: $4,188/yr Ruzuku $199/mo Pro ($199/mo): $199/mo 0% platform fee (standard Stripe 2.9% + $0.30) Annual: $2,388/yr Save $150/mo ($1,800/yr) with Ruzuku What you get on Thinkific - Remove branding - API & webhooks - PayPal payments - Phone & priority support - 3 communities - Group orders & invoicing What you'll still need - 1% Stripe surcharge = $200/mo on $20K revenue - Still 10,000 student cap - No SCORM (requires Plus) - Mobile app = extra $199/mo - No student tech support ## Thinkific vs. Ruzuku: Honest Comparison A fair, side-by-side cost comparison focused on what matters most to course creators. | Feature | Thinkific | Ruzuku | | --- | --- | --- | | Starting price (annual) | $36/mo (Basic) | $83/mo (Core annual) | | Transaction fees (own Stripe) | 1-5% surcharge on top of Stripe | 0% — use your own Stripe freely | | Courses | Unlimited | Unlimited | | Students | 10,000 on all standard plans | Unlimited | | Payment plans | Start ($74/mo) and up | All paid plans | | Certificates | Start ($74/mo) and up | Pro ($199/mo) | | Student tech support | Not included | Included on all paid plans | | Mobile app | $199/mo add-on | Not available | | Native Zoom integration | Start ($74/mo) and up | All paid plans | | Custom branding removal | Grow ($149/mo) | Pro ($199/mo) | ### Choose Thinkific if: You need a deep course builder with extensive customization, want built-in email automation and landing pages, or need SCORM compliance (on Plus). Thinkific's feature set is one of the deepest in the market if you're willing to use TCommerce to avoid surcharges. ### Choose Ruzuku if: You want unlimited students without a 10,000 cap, zero payment processor surcharges regardless of which Stripe account you use, built-in student tech support, and payment plans on your first paid plan without upgrading to a $74/mo tier. ## Thinkific Pricing FAQ ### Does Thinkific charge transaction fees? Thinkific charges 0% if you use TCommerce (their built-in processor). If you use your own Stripe, Thinkific adds a surcharge: 5% on Basic ($36/mo annual), 2% on Start ($74/mo), and 1% on Grow ($149/mo). Standard Stripe processing fees (2.9% + $0.30) apply on top of any surcharge. ### How many students can I have on Thinkific? All three standard plans (Basic, Start, and Grow) cap you at 10,000 current students. This is often overlooked since Thinkific emphasizes 'unlimited courses.' Unlimited students requires the Plus plan (custom pricing). ### Is Thinkific worth it in 2026? Thinkific is worth it if you need deep course building tools, want SCORM support (on Plus), or are comfortable using TCommerce to avoid surcharges. The Start plan at $74/mo (annual) is the sweet spot — it unlocks certificates, assignments, payment plans, memberships, and Zoom integration. ### Does Thinkific have a free plan? No. Thinkific removed its free plan. New users can start a free trial, then plans begin at $36/month (annual billing) or $49/month (monthly). ### What is the Thinkific Payments surcharge? If you don't use TCommerce (Thinkific's built-in processor) and instead connect your own Stripe, Thinkific charges an additional surcharge per transaction: 5% on Basic, 2% on Start, 1% on Grow. On the Basic plan with your own Stripe, you'd pay Stripe's 2.9% plus Thinkific's 5% = 7.9% total per transaction. ### Can I switch from Thinkific to another platform? You can export student lists and download individual video files. There's no one-click course export. Active subscriptions through TCommerce cannot be migrated. Courses, page designs, and automations need to be rebuilt. Budget 2-4 weeks for a full migration. ### How much does Thinkific cost per month? Thinkific has three standard plans: Basic at $49/month ($36/month billed annually), Start at $99/month ($74/month annually), and Grow at $199/month ($149/month annually). There is also a Plus plan with custom pricing for enterprise needs. All three standard plans cap you at 10,000 students. ### Are Thinkific coupon codes legit? Most Thinkific coupon codes found online are not legitimate. Third-party coupon sites list fabricated or expired codes — Thinkific does not distribute public promo codes. The only official savings are the 14-day free trial and annual billing discount (up to 27% off). ### What is the best way to get a discount on Thinkific? Choose annual billing to save 25-27% on any plan. Start with the free 14-day trial to test the platform. There are no legitimate public coupon codes. If Thinkific's Stripe surcharges are a concern, Ruzuku offers zero processor surcharges on all plans with a permanent free tier to start. ## Compare Thinkific to Ruzuku Start free and see the difference. Zero transaction fees, unlimited courses, unlimited students — no credit card required. [Start Free Today](https://courses.ruzuku.com/onboarding/guides) [Book a Course Clarity Call](https://courses.ruzuku.com/meet/petersmiley/ruzuku-consult) No credit card required · 0% transaction fees · Student support included

# Thinkific Pricing Plans 2025: Which Plan Is Right For You?

![Thinkific Pricing Plans Which Plan Is Right For You](https://cdn.elearningindustry.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/10/Thinkific-Pricing-Plans-Which-Plan-Is-Right-For-You.jpg)

Roman Samborskyi/Shutterstock.com


Summary:

Thinkific offers many pricing plans, each with its own features. Read this detailed guide on Thinkific pricing and decide what's the best option for your business needs.


## About Thinkific

If you want to learn more about Thinkific and all the details about the Thinkific pricing plans, you're in the right place. Let's start by saying that this platform is all about making you feel like an expert when it comes to building and selling online courses. Why's that? Because Thinkific gives you all the tools you need in order to create, market, and sell your own courses without necessarily being tech-savvy. You can customize your course website, make your content stand out, and even create quizzes, assignments, and certificates for your students. Also, keep in mind that Thinkific doesn't just help you create courses; it's got tools to promote them, too, like SEO optimization, email campaigns, integrations with payment systems, etc.

## Latest Thinkific Pricing: Quick Overview

Thinkific's pricing plans are designed to accommodate everyone, from those who engage with course creation for the first time to those who are pros. Below, you will find an overview of the [Thinkific](https://elearningindustry.com/directory/elearning-software/thinkific "Thinkific software") pricing plans so you can choose what works best for you.

Next, you can check out the **Start** plan, with an original price of $99 per month, which has all the Basic plan's features but also adds memberships and advanced integrations. There's also the **Grow** plan at $199 per month, which has all the advanced tools that are perfect for scaling your business and expanding into multiple learning products. The **Expand** plan is the most expensive one, at $499 per month, and is perfect for teams, as it offers more admin accounts. Lastly, Thinkific pricing includes the **Plus** plan, which is intended for enterprises and has custom pricing models. Let's dive deeper into each Thinkific pricing plan.

## Thinkific Pricing Comparison

### Thinkific Free Plan

#### Key Features Of The Free Plan

The first key feature of the plan is that it's free. However, as we've already mentioned, it has its limits. Still, you can create just one course with unlimited students, which is a good start if you want to test your content. You also get access to a drag-and-drop course builder, meaning you can create your course easily, even if you're a beginner. The Free plan also comes with the ability to upload a variety of multimedia content, such as video, audio, PDFs, and quizzes. This allows you to make your lessons engaging rather than just offering boring text-based modules. Plus, you can use email support in case you encounter problems. But what about learner assessments? Although limited, the Thinkific Free pricing plan allows you to add surveys and quizzes to review your audience's progress.

#### What's Not So Great?

As mentioned above, the free Thinkific pricing plan offers a single course, so if you plan to create multiple courses, you'll need to upgrade to another pricing plan eventually. The customization options are also a bit limited. You can change the basics, of course, but you won't get access to the full variety of themes or the more advanced design features that are available in the paid plans. Probably the most significant disadvantage is the fact that you can't remove Thinkific's branding on the Free plan. So, if you want to add your own logo or branding to your course and fully own it, you'll need to pay for another plan. Additionally, there's no access to advanced marketing tools, which might make it more difficult for you to expand your student base.

#### Who Can Benefit From Thinkific's Free Plan?

Thinkific's Free plan is perfect for beginners or those who see course creation as a hobby. It's also a great fit for educators or professionals who want to offer a single high-quality course. This Thinkific pricing plan gives them all the tools to start or test their content to see if it works before scaling up.

#### Free Plan Demo: Video Lessons With Content

The Free Thinkific pricing plan allows you to test your lessons. You can easily upload and organize your video lessons and add content like PDFs or quizzes, too. Just like that, you get a demo of a mini-course with a couple of short video lessons to see what your courses will look like, all on a free platform.

### Thinkific Basic Plan At $49 Per Month ($36 Per Month If Billed Annually)

#### Key Features Of The Basic Plan

This plan has gathered some great features, especially for its price. Unlike the Free plan, you can create unlimited courses, which is appealing enough to make you choose it. You also get to use your own domain name, making the website that hosts your courses look more professional. This Thinkific pricing plan also offers email support, so if you ever need help, their team is just an email away. What's more, you can add coupons and promo codes to boost sales, release your content gradually, send your learners automated emails, and group your courses into bundles to earn more.

#### What's Not So Great?

The Thinkific Basic plan has its own drawbacks. First, unlike the more expensive plans, you can't host live lessons, which isn't ideal if you're looking to interact with students in real time. And speaking of interactions with students, this Thinkific pricing plan doesn't give you access to Thinkific Communities, a feature that allows you to build a learning community around your course. Lastly, same as the Free plan, you can't add your own logo to your courses.

#### Who Can Benefit From Thinkific's Basic Plan?

This plan is just right for beginners who want their courses to look professional. It's also great for small businesses that want to train their employees or individuals who are looking for extra income. Coaches and educators, in general, can also benefit from this plan if they want to offer simple but quality courses without fancy add-ons.

### Thinkific Start Plan At $99 Per Month ($74 Per Month When Billed Annually)

#### Key Features Of The Start Plan

The Thinkific Start plan comes with many features that aim to help you scale up and add more elements to your online courses. First off, you get access to live lessons and webinars. This means that you can now engage with your students in real time and build an interactive learning experience. You also get to create memberships and subscriptions, which gives you the opportunity to have recurring revenue. Now, it also adds more payment plans. Whether you want to offer one-time payments, custom pricing models, or even coupons, now you can. The plan also includes integrations with email marketing apps.

#### What's Not So Great?

While this Thinkific pricing plan offers many features, it still lacks some more advanced options. For example, you can't get advanced analytics or remove the Thinkific logo. Most importantly, though, the price ($99/month) may feel expensive to those who are just starting out.

#### Who Can Benefit From Thinkific's Start Plan?

If you're already running courses and looking for ways to boost engagement, build a community through memberships, or introduce more flexible payment options, the Thinkific Start plan is for you. It's also useful for coaches, consultants, and small business owners who want to offer live lessons and create ongoing content for their subscribers.

### Thinkific Grow Plan At $199 Per Month ($149 Per Month When Billed Annually)

#### Key Features Of The Grow Plan

With the Grow plan, you get everything from the Start plan, plus many more powerful tools, like the ability to Thinkific's branding and replace it with your own. This gives your business a more professional appearance, which is necessary if you're working with other companies or institutions. This Thinkific pricing plan allows you bulk enrollments, which basically means that if a company wants to sign up all its employees at once, now it's possible. With the Grow plan, you also have access to more customization options, advanced reporting on learner progress, priority at support, and, of course, learning communities.

#### What's Not So Great?

Of course, $199 a month is a big investment, which may hurt a company's budget if they don't fully utilize all the features. However, the investment could pay off in the future. Additionally, while the customization options are great, they require some coding knowledge. If you don't have a developer around to help you, it might get a bit overwhelming. What's more, Thinkific's marketing tools are limited compared to other platforms. This results in needing external tools to get your work done.

#### Who Can Benefit From Thinkific's Grow Plan?

This plan is ideal for professional course creators and businesses with a large number of learners who need branding over their courses and require more advanced reporting and customization. So, if you're looking for more flexibility and advanced tools, this Thinkific pricing plan is exactly what you need.

### Thinkific Expand Plan At $499 A Month ($374 When Billed Annually)

#### Key Features Of The Expand Plan

The Thinkific Expand plan is the ultimate tool to build an education empire. If you have a big team, no other plan can give you up to 15 different admin accounts. This means that your instructors, tech support, and marketing team can all access the platform with individual permissions. Even better, Thinkific offers personalized onboarding and training services to help large businesses get started and be productive immediately. You'll also have access to more tools for customizing your [learning platform](https://elearningindustry.com/directory/software-categories/learning-management-systems "Learning Management Systems (LMS)") so it reflects your brand perfectly.

#### What's Not So Great?

While the Thinkific Expand plan offers tons of features, it's not without its cons. For instance, features like API access might be too complicated or unnecessary for users who can't deal with more advanced tech integrations. If you don't have a dedicated team to manage this, you may not get the full value. Also, if your business is small, you might feel like you're paying for tools that you don't need just yet until you scale up. In that case, the lower-tier plans might be a better fit.

#### Who Can Benefit From Thinkific's Expand Plan?

So, who is this plan really for? It's ideal for mid-to-large-sized online education businesses, organizations that are expanding quickly and need features like multiple admin accounts, educational institutions, large training companies, businesses with multiple products, and large teams. If your business needs to build communities, integrate the platform with other systems, or customize learners' experiences, this Thinkific pricing plan will help you achieve all your goals.

### Thinkific Plus Plan (Custom Rates)

#### Key Features Of The Plus Plan

Unlike other Thinkific pricing tiers, which come with standard fees per month, the Plus plan offers custom rates. This means pricing is tailored specifically to your business's unique needs, whether you're growing fast or handling large volumes of students. But it's not just the pricing flexibility that makes it special. It allows you to add your own branding to your courses and gives you API access to integrate Thinkific with your CRM or more complicated apps. Most importantly, though, it offers you dedicated support with your own Customer Success Manager, who's aware of your business needs and can offer targeted help. Not to mention the single sign-on (SSO) system which lets learners use existing login credentials, making it easier for them to access your content without creating new accounts.

#### What's Not So Great?

Those features are amazing, but many may be unnecessary or costly for small businesses. For instance, API access is useful but requires technical knowledge to operate. Plus, although you can customize your platform's features and pay accordingly, you're bound to end up with tools you won't need, as they come in bundles.

#### Who Can Benefit From Thinkific's Plus Plan?

The short answer is corporate training organizations. The ability to create private, branded learning modules and integrate them with existing systems makes Thinkific Plus perfect for delivering professional development or compliance training. Universities and institutions can also consider this Thinkific pricing plan, as they can manage multiple courses and departments more easily this way. In addition, as it can get costly to get the full pack with all the premium features, this plan best suits you if your courses are already expensive, so you can quickly get Return On Investment.

### What To Consider When Choosing A Thinkific Pricing Plan

When deciding on the best Thinkific pricing plan, consider your business size, course volume, and goals. For starters, the Free plan is great if you're just testing your abilities in course creation, while the Basic plan works well for creators looking for customization and monetization options. If you need more advanced tools, like app integrations and memberships, the Start or Grow plans offer more flexibility. Lastly, don't forget the custom Thinkific pricing plan, Plus, which is ideal for high-volume businesses needing branding, SSO, and more targeted support.

### Is There A Thinkific Alternative?

There are plenty of Thinkific alternatives, depending on what you're looking for. Kajabi, [Teachable](https://elearningindustry.com/directory/elearning-software/teachable "Teachable software"), and Podia are some popular options that offer similar course-building features. Yet, each has its pros and cons in terms of pricing, ease of use, and customization. When it comes to [Thinkific pricing](https://elearningindustry.com/directory/elearning-software/thinkific/pricing "Thinkific Pricing"), it offers a free plan, which sets it apart from competitors like [Kajabi](https://elearningindustry.com/directory/elearning-software/kajabi "Kajabi software"), which starts at a higher monthly fee. If you're comparing features and budgets, Thinkific might still be the most valuable option for many users.

### Final Thoughts On Thinkific Pricing

When it comes to Thinkific pricing, there's something for everyone, whether you're just starting or you are a pro who manages multiple courses. From the Free plan to custom rates in the Plus plan, Thinkific adjusts to your needs as your business grows. The flexibility of each pricing plan allows you to find the perfect fit based on your needs and budget.

### Thinkific Pricing Plans FAQs

#### Can I use Thinkific for free?

Yes. Thinkific offers a free plan that gives you access to limited but basic features, like hosting courses and engaging with your learners.

#### Does Thinkific charge transaction fees?

Thinkific Payments partners with Stripe to process payments. If you're in the US or Canada, you'll be charged a fee of 2.9% plus 30 cents for every successful credit card payment. The transaction fees vary based on the country where your business operates. For more detailed information on transaction fees, visit the Thinkific Help Center.

#### What is the difference between Thinkific Basic and Start?

The Basic plan gives you all the essential features, such as unlimited courses and a custom domain, for $49 per month. The Start plan adds more advanced features. It's perfect if you want to make your content more exclusive, as it offers membership and payment plan functionality, and it costs $99 per month.

#### How many courses can you host on Thinkific?

Unlimited with any paid pricing plan.

#### Is it possible to download my course from Thinkific?

Unfortunately, you can't download a full course. However, you can save your videos and PDFs from Thinkific directly in the Course Builder.

#### What is the maximum upload size for Thinkific?

Each file can be up to 2 GB. It's enough for high-quality content of any kind.

#### Can Thinkific be used as a website?

Yes. Thinkific allows you to create a fully branded site with pages for your courses and blogs.

#### Does Thinkific have a money-back guarantee?

Thinkific does not provide refunds unless an error was made on its part.

#### Do I need to pay for anything else?

In addition to the Thinkific pricing plan of your choice, you may have to pay extra for integrated apps or custom domains in some tiers.

Originally published on October 3, 2024

# Thinkific Reviews, Alternatives, Pricing, & Offerings in 2026

[![](https://www.myengineeringbuddy.com/blog/wp-content/uploads/2025/04/T2367hem-96x96.webp)](https://www.myengineeringbuddy.com/blog/author/kumar-hemendra/)

By [Kumar Hemendra](https://www.myengineeringbuddy.com/blog/author/kumar-hemendra/ "Posts by Kumar Hemendra")\|Last Updated: March 11, 2026\|

#### [Contents](https://www.myengineeringbuddy.com/blog/thinkific-reviews-alternatives-pricing-offerings/\#033f987c22185d21f)

- [Thinkific Reviews and Testimonials](https://www.myengineeringbuddy.com/blog/thinkific-reviews-alternatives-pricing-offerings/#toc_Thinkific_Reviews_and_Testimonials)
- [Thinkific Pricing](https://www.myengineeringbuddy.com/blog/thinkific-reviews-alternatives-pricing-offerings/#toc_Thinkific_Pricing)
- [Thinkific Alternatives](https://www.myengineeringbuddy.com/blog/thinkific-reviews-alternatives-pricing-offerings/#toc_Thinkific_Alternatives)
- [How It Works?](https://www.myengineeringbuddy.com/blog/thinkific-reviews-alternatives-pricing-offerings/#toc_How_It_Works)
- [FAQs About Thinkific](https://www.myengineeringbuddy.com/blog/thinkific-reviews-alternatives-pricing-offerings/#toc_FAQs_About_Thinkific)

[**Thinkific**](https://www.thinkific.com/) is a popular online course platform that lets experts create and sell training programs. It’s widely used by entrepreneurs and businesses to deliver online learning. This article gives students and parents information on Thinkific, covering user reviews, pricing, and how it works, so they can compare it with other tutoring and course platforms.

![](https://myengineeringbuddy.com/blog/wp-content/uploads/2025/09/thinkific.home_.webp)

We will look at Thinkific’s reputation, costs, and services in 2025, and compare it to alternatives like Teachable, Podia, and My Engineering Buddy. The goal is to help advanced-level students make an informed decision when choosing a learning platform.

To understand how Thinkific supports your teaching business, look at the ecosystem below. It combines three critical functions—course building, payments, and marketing—into one dashboard.

![Concept map showing Thinkific platform features including Course Builder, Payments, Marketing tools, and Student Site customization.](https://www.myengineeringbuddy.com/blog/wp-content/uploads/2025/10/thinkific-features-concept-map-01.webp)

The Thinkific ecosystem combines course creation, secure payments, and marketing tools into one dashboard.

This integrated approach allows you to focus entirely on creating content while the platform handles the technical backend.

## Thinkific Reviews and Testimonials

### Trustpilot (840 reviews, 2.7/5)

Thinkific has 840 reviews on [Trustpilot with an average](https://www.trustpilot.com/review/thinkific.com) rating of about 2.7 out of 5. The Trustpilot summary shows most users were unhappy with their experience. Common complaints include poor customer support and billing issues – for example, many say they were charged even after canceling their accounts. Some users praise features like free trials or digital download options, but the majority express frustration over cancellations and refunds.

G2 (368 reviews, 4.5/5)

On [G2, Thinkific has](https://www.g2.com/products/thinkific/reviews) 368 reviews with an average 4.5/5 rating. G2 users often highlight how easy Thinkific is to use. One review notes it is “really easy to use even if you are not a technological boffin,” and praises its great features and responsive support. In general, G2 reviewers love the intuitive course builder, no-code setup, and helpful support team. Negative comments on G2 are minor (such as limited features on basic plans), so overall the G2 feedback is very positive.

User satisfaction varies significantly depending on the review platform. The chart below visualizes the disparity between technical satisfaction (G2/Capterra) and customer service complaints (Trustpilot).

![Bar chart comparing Thinkific user ratings: G2 (4.5/5), Capterra (4.4/5), and Trustpilot (2.7/5).](https://www.myengineeringbuddy.com/blog/wp-content/uploads/2025/10/thinkific-user-reviews-chart-02.webp)

User ratings vary significantly by platform, with G2 and Capterra users reporting high satisfaction compared to Trustpilot.

While the software itself is highly rated for ease of use, be aware that billing and support issues are the primary drivers of negative feedback.

### Capterra (193 reviews, 4.4/5)

[Capterra reports](https://www.capterra.com/p/132888/Thinkific/) 193 user reviews with an average rating of 4.4/5 for Thinkific. According to Capterra, Thinkific stands out for its \*\*easy course setup\*\* and built-in tools. The summary says it is ideal for teams that want to launch courses without technical skills. Reviewers on Capterra praise features like AI course prompts and a new mobile app, but also note some downsides: customization options can be limited, and support can be slower. Overall, Capterra views Thinkific as a practical, cost-effective LMS for small teams, with trade-offs in advanced features.

### Their Own Website

[Thinkific’s official website](https://mitchasser.com/thinkific-review-do-not-use-it/) shares success stories and stats, but not user ratings. It boasts that 35,000+ customers have earned $3.7 billion by teaching courses on Thinkific. These case studies show users who built real businesses, but they are marketing material rather than independent reviews. In short, Thinkific’s site claims many achievements (like 214 million total learners), but for honest feedback one should look at the review sites above.

**Is Thinkific legit?** Based on these sources, yes. Thinkific is a legitimate, well-established platform with thousands of course creators. It has strong positive ratings on G2 and Capterra, indicating that [many users trus](https://www.reddit.com/r/Entrepreneur/comments/cuuw91/anyone_ever_used_thinkific_for_an_online_course/) t it. The Trustpilot scores are lower, but those reflect service complaints rather than a scam. In summary, Thinkific is a real company with solid technology, though users should be aware of potential support and billing issues.

## Thinkific Pricing

### Pricing Range

Thinkific [uses a subscription model](https://www.thinkific.com/pricing/) with no per-hour fees. Instructors pay a monthly or annual rate. The Basic plan starts at $49 per month (when billed monthly) or $39 per month (with annual billing). The higher plans are $99/mo (Start) and $199/mo (Grow) on monthly billing. (Annual billing saves about 25%.) There is also an enterprise option (Thinkific Plus) with custom pricing. Unlike tutoring marketplaces, Thinkific does not take a commission on course sales – instructors keep all revenue, aside from any Stripe/ [PayPal processing](https://www.paypal.com/in/home) fees. For details of each plan and what they include, see Thinkific’s pricing page}.

Comparing the tiers can be confusing, but the most important feature—transaction fees—remains constant. Here is a breakdown of the three main paid plans.

![Comparison table of Thinkific Basic ($49), Start ($99), and Grow ($199) plans showing 0% transaction fees on all.](https://www.myengineeringbuddy.com/blog/wp-content/uploads/2025/10/thinkific-pricing-plan-comparison-03.webp)

Unlike many competitors, Thinkific charges 0% transaction fees across all paid plans, maximizing your revenue.

The standout feature here is the **0% transaction fee** across all paid plans, meaning you keep 100% of your sales revenue (minus standard credit card processing fees).

### What Students Say About Pricing

[Users generally find](https://courses.thinkific.com/signup?plan=medium_22&interval=annual) Thinkific’s pricing transparent. Reviewers note that Thinkific clearly shows plan costs and has no hidden fees. However, beginners sometimes feel the plans can be pricey, since there is no permanent free tier. (Thinkific only offers a 30-day trial; after that a paid plan is required.) Compared to similar platforms (which often charge $29–$150 per month for basic plans), Thinkific’s pricing is in the same range. Many reviewers appreciate that Thinkific does not charge extra transaction fees, making it “budget-friendly” in that sense. In short, customers say Thinkific’s cost is fair for the features offered, but it can seem high if you need advanced features or no free plan.

### Hidden Costs

Thinkific does not charge registration fees or cancellation fees. But instructors should be aware of payment-processing costs. Credit card transactions incur the standard ~2.9% + $0.30 fee. Also, if you use an external gateway (Stripe/ [PayPal](https://www.paypal.com/in/home)) instead of Thinkific’s built-in payment system, Thinkific adds a small transaction fee on top (up to 5% on Basic plan sales, 2% on Start, 1% on Grow, waived beyond the first $1M yearly). These fees are clearly stated, but they do add to the cost of selling courses. There are no other hidden charges – taxes like VAT apply as normal, and you can cancel at any time without penalty.

### How Thinkific’s Pricing Works

Instructors choose monthly or yearly billing for a plan. [Students buy courses by paying](https://www.thinkific.com/pricing/) online. Thinkific accepts major credit cards (via Stripe/Thinkific Payments) and PayPal. Tutors set course prices themselves, and Thinkific handles payment collection. The platform allows creating bundles, subscriptions, coupons, and payment plans (if the tutor offers them). For example, instructors can bundle multiple courses together or offer bulk discounts. Thinkific also supports group purchases: on the Grow plan you can sell bulk licenses or bulk enrollments for teams. There are no special student discounts (like military or student rates) built in. Promotional coupons can be created by tutors for their own courses.

### Free Trial

Thinkific provides a 30-day free trial for all new users. During this time, [you get full access to the platform](https://www.thinkific.com/thinkific-demo/) – you can build courses, launch a site, and test features without paying. No credit card is required upfront. After 30 days, you must switch to a paid plan to continue using Thinkific. (Note: Thinkific does not have a permanent free plan. The trial is the only free option.)

### Refund Policy

According to Thinkific’s terms, they [do not give refunds](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360056382214-Thinkific-Payments-Refunds) except when they make a mistake. In other words, the official policy is “no refunds” by default. In reality, many users complain that refunds are very hard to get. Trustpilot reviews show people being charged after canceling and then being denied refunds. This suggests Thinkific enforces its no-refund policy strictly. If a user is unhappy and cancels, they should not expect their payment back. Thinkific’s refund policy is minimal, so it’s important to be sure before you pay.

![](https://myengineeringbuddy.com/blog/wp-content/uploads/2025/09/Thinkific-refund.webp)

## Thinkific Alternatives

Students or parents might look at alternatives if Thinkific lacks certain features or is not the right fit. Below are some popular competitors, including My Engineering Buddy (MEB), which offers tutoring services. Each alternative has its own strengths and weaknesses in pricing, features, and tutor quality.

How does Thinkific stack up against major course-hosting rivals like Teachable and Kajabi? This comparison highlights the key differences in fees and marketing tools.

![Comparison matrix showing Thinkific vs Teachable vs Kajabi on fees, free plans, and marketing tools.](https://www.myengineeringbuddy.com/blog/wp-content/uploads/2025/10/thinkific-vs-teachable-kajabi-comparison-04.webp)

While Thinkific wins on transaction fees, Kajabi offers superior marketing tools for a higher price.

While Kajabi offers better marketing automation, Thinkific is the clear winner for cost-conscious creators who want to avoid transaction fees.

### Teachable

![](https://myengineeringbuddy.com/blog/wp-content/uploads/2025/09/Teachable-home.webp)

- **Pricing:** [Plans start around](https://teachable.com/) $29–$39 per month, up to $179 for higher tiers. The lowest plan charges a 5% transaction fee. This is cheaper than Thinkific’s Basic price, but the starter plan has limited features (e.g. no fixed free plan).
- **Features:** Teachable has a good course builder and supports unlimited courses. It includes built-in affiliate marketing and a mobile app. It is easy to use for beginners.
- **Pros over Thinkific:** Slightly lower starting cost, strong affiliate tools, simple interface, marketplace visibility (courses can appear on Teachable’s marketplace).
- **Cons:** Transaction fee on low plans, fewer customization options, no community pages or memberships in basic plans. It lacks some of Thinkific’s advanced site-building features.

### Podia

![](https://myengineeringbuddy.com/blog/wp-content/uploads/2025/09/Podia-home.webp)

- **Pricing:** [Podia plans](https://www.podia.com/) run about $39–$89 per month, with no transaction fees. It offers a 14-day free trial. Unlike Thinkific, Podia does not have a free tier.
- **Features:** Podia lets you sell courses, digital downloads, memberships, and webinars all in one place. It has strong email marketing and messaging tools built-in.
- **Pros:** Very user-friendly, includes email/newsletter and simple site builder. Unlimited courses and students on all plans, with no extra fees. Good support and education resources.
- **Cons:** Simpler feature set than Thinkific: it lacks quizzes, certificates, and detailed analytics. Fewer layout options and design settings. Can be too basic for very large or complex course businesses.

### [My Engineering Buddy](https://www.myengineeringbuddy.com/)

![](https://myengineeringbuddy.com/blog/wp-content/uploads/2025/05/MyEngineeringBuddy.com_-1.webp)

- **Pricing:** MEB [charges by the hour](https://www.myengineeringbuddy.com/pricing/) (often in the range of $20–$50/hr) for one-on-one tutoring. This can be cheaper than a full platform subscription if you only need occasional help. There is no monthly membership fee on MEB.
- **Subjects Covered:** MEB specializes in engineering, math, and advanced STEM subjects. It offers live help with homework and exam prep. Thinkific, by contrast, is for self-paced courses on any topic.
- **Pros:** Personalized attention – each student gets a dedicated tutor. Tutors are usually highly qualified (often PhD or industry experts). Quick feedback and interactive sessions. Flexible scheduling to fit student needs.
- **Cons:** MEB is not a course marketplace; it’s tutoring services. It has no course library or site-building tools. If you need to access recorded courses or comprehensive learning materials on your own schedule, MEB doesn’t provide that.

### Kajabi

![](https://myengineeringbuddy.com/blog/wp-content/uploads/2025/09/Kajabi-homr.webp)

- **Pricing:** [Kajabi’s plans start around](https://kajabi.com/?utm_campaign=home_trial&utm_content=2148107523&utm_medium=affiliate&utm_source=L+L) $149/month and go higher (often 3–4 times more than Thinkific). It has no transaction fees, but the high cost can be a barrier.
- **Features:** Kajabi is an all-in-one solution for courses, marketing, and sales. It includes course hosting, landing pages, email marketing, affiliate management, and community features. It also has excellent analytics and automation.
- **Pros:** Powerful built-in tools for marketing (funnels, email, CRM). Highly customizable design. No technical skills required for marketing automations.
- **Cons:** Very expensive for small creators. Steeper learning curve. Many creators feel it is overkill unless they need the full marketing stack. Thinkific offers a simpler interface at a lower price.

### LearnWorlds

![](https://myengineeringbuddy.com/blog/wp-content/uploads/2025/09/Learnworlds-homr.webp)

- **Pricing:** [Plans start around](https://www.learnworlds.com/?utm_medium=affiliate&utm_campaign=hungtran3470&utm_source=partners&pscd=get.learnworlds.com&ps_partner_key=aHVuZ3RyYW4zNDcw&ps_xid=qR34HLjONp5yw3&gsxid=qR34HLjONp5yw3&gspk=aHVuZ3RyYW4zNDcw&gad_source=1&gad_campaignid=22520158316&gbraid=0AAAAA_iqut5VeliNV8xdq0NfWVNNQZNw2&gclid=Cj0KCQjw3OjGBhDYARIsADd-uX4wC_Jud-JBv3Qwo_k1XfqygROWdipqVPMYyLBrfKv9xD8MzF-dmUAaAraVEALw_wcB) $29–$99/month. It offers a free trial and basic plans cheaper than Thinkific’s Grow tier. Higher plans add advanced features (like certificates and custom domains).
- **Features:** LearnWorlds is strong on interactive learning. It supports SCORM courses, heavy customization, and built-in analytics. It also has social/community features and good certification options.
- **Pros:** Great for organizations needing corporate-style training and assessments. Lots of e-learning features (e.g. quizzes, exams, certificates). Offers a site builder and various integrations.
- **Cons:** The interface can be complex and less intuitive for new users. It may have more features than a solo instructor needs, making it feel complicated. Thinkific is simpler and more straightforward in comparison.

### Nas.io

![](https://www.myengineeringbuddy.com/blog/wp-content/uploads/2025/10/Nas.io-Homepage-screenshot.webp)

- **Pricing:** [Nas.io](https://nas.io/) offers flexible pricing depending on the tools and features used, with options designed to be accessible for solopreneurs and small teams. It focuses on affordability for creators who want to launch without high upfront costs or a dedicated marketing budget.
- **Features**: Nas.io combines AI-powered lead generation, a one-click landing page builder, email marketing, ads and targeting tools, payment processing, and product delivery in one system. It supports selling courses, workshops, sessions, challenges, guides, and other digital products, all within a streamlined dashboard.
- **Pros:** Ideal for beginners and non-technical users who want an all-in-one solution. Users can attract customers even without an existing audience, thanks to built-in AI and marketing tools. Simple setup and automation make it suitable for busy creators, coaches, and consultants.
- **Cons:** It may not offer the same depth of advanced course customization or SCORM support that traditional LMS-focused platforms provide. Instructors looking for enterprise-level academic features may find Thinkific or similar platforms more specialized.”

## How It Works?

### For Students

Students [begin by creating a free Thinkific](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360030719433-Getting-Started-with-Thinkific) account. They enter their name and email, and confirm their account via an email link. Once signed up, students browse available courses (usually via a website or direct link). [To enroll in a course](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us), a student clicks on the course and pays (if it is paid). The course then appears in their account dashboard.

Thinkific does not match students with tutors directly. Instead, students choose a course and proceed. Communication with the instructor happens inside the course: [many courses have](https://www.thinkific.com/features/courses/) Q&A sections, discussion forums, or messaging tools where students can ask questions. If a course includes live sessions or coaching, students will find a booking calendar and select a time slot.

Scheduling is mostly set by the course content. Self-paced courses have no schedule – students learn on their own timeline. For live components (like [webinars or one-on-one coaching](https://webinars.thinkific.com/)), the instructor sets available times, and the student picks one. Thinkific then sends a confirmation and reminders (often via email) and provides a link (like a Zoom link) for the session. Overall, the process is user-friendly: sign up, enroll, and start learning with minimal steps.

### For Tutors

Starting your own online academy might seem daunting, but the process is actually quite linear. Follow this roadmap to launch your first course.

![5-step flowchart for Thinkific tutors: Sign up, Customize Site, Upload Content, Set Pricing, Publish and Earn.](https://www.myengineeringbuddy.com/blog/wp-content/uploads/2025/10/thinkific-tutor-launch-process-flowchart-05.webp)

Launching a course is a linear process: build your site, upload content, and set your price to start earning.

Once you reach step 5 and publish, your primary focus shifts from creation to marketing your link to potential students.

Becoming a tutor (instructor) on Thinkific is straightforward. Anyone can sign up to teach. [First, go to Thinkific’s website](https://www.thinkific.com/blog/how-to-become-an-online-coach/) and click to get started (see the Pricing page for plan options). You register with your email and create an account. Next, you choose a subscription plan and enter billing details. After signing up, set up your teaching site by adding your branding (name, logo) in the site builder.

Now you build your course. In the admin dashboard, you use Thinkific’s tools to add course content: video lessons, text, quizzes, downloads, etc. For each course you create, you choose a title, description, and price or coupon. You can also set up other products like memberships or bundles. Thinkific handles [hosting and payments](https://www.thinkific.com/features/thinkific-payments/), so you only need to focus on content.

Once your course is ready, you publish it. Students can then enroll and pay through Thinkific’s checkout. You receive the revenue (minus standard payment fees). Throughout this process, Thinkific provides step-by-step guides and AI tools (like an outline generator) to help you build content. There is no formal approval needed – as long as you follow the rules, your course goes live.

Tutors set their own course fees; Thinkific does not fix tutor rates. You can change or cancel your plan at any time. [Thinkific offers 24/7 access](https://courses.thinkific.com/onboarding/signin) to course creation tools. To learn more or get started, see Thinkific’s official pricing and sign-up page, which guides you through choosing a plan.

## [FAQs About Thinkific](https://www.thinkific.com/faq/)

**Q: How does Thinkific compare to My Engineering Buddy?** Thinkific is a self-service course platform, whereas My Engineering Buddy (MEB) provides live one-on-one tutoring in [engineering and math](https://www.myengineeringbuddy.com/online-tutoring/online-math-tutoring/). Thinkific charges fixed monthly subscriptions and is designed for selling courses; MEB charges by tutoring sessions and focuses on homework help. For complex STEM subjects needing personal guidance, MEB offers direct instructor support. Thinkific is better for hosting and selling courses on various topics, but MEB is better for individual tutoring in advanced subjects.

**Q: Can tutors set up their own fee or is it fixed by the company?** Instructors on Thinkific set their own prices. Thinkific does not assign fixed rates – each tutor (course creator) chooses how much to charge for their courses or tutoring sessions.

**Q: How much can a tutor expect to earn on an hourly basis?** Thinkific instructors earn based on course sales, not a guaranteed hourly rate. Earnings vary widely – one instructor might make a few dollars from a low-priced course, while a popular course could earn thousands. There is no standard hourly earning, since income depends on how many students buy your courses.

**Q: How many hours of work can a tutor get per month?** There is no guaranteed number of hours or students. Workload depends on how well you promote your courses and how much demand there is. Some instructors might only get a few enrollments (part-time work), while others with popular courses can teach many hours or earn full-time income. It is up to each tutor’s marketing and content quality.

**Q: Is it easy to get students on Thinkific?** Not necessarily. Thinkific is a platform for hosting courses, but it does not provide a built-in marketplace or student matchmaking. Instructors must promote their courses through social media, ads, [blogs, or email](https://www.thinkific.com/blog/) lists. Without marketing effort, getting students can be challenging. Thinkific does offer tools (SEO for courses, coupon codes, affiliate features) to help reach students, but it takes work by the tutor to attract learners.

**Q: Tips on how to get more students on Thinkific:** Promote your courses outside Thinkific: use social media, content marketing, and email newsletters to reach learners. Offer coupons or discounts to first-time students, and consider running webinars or free previews to draw interest. Use Thinkific’s affiliate program to have others recommend your course. Collect and show student testimonials to build trust. Also, continually improve your course content and SEO-friendly descriptions to rank better in search results.

**Q: What tutors like about Thinkific:** Tutors often praise Thinkific’s ease of use and features. One user said “Thinkific is really easy to use… it has great features and the support is awesome”. Instructors like that they can build courses without any tech skills and still have professional tools. Many appreciate the 30-day free trial and that Thinkific handles the tech side of payments. People also note that the course and website builders work well, and the integrated tools (like quizzes and communities) are useful. According to user reviews, the platform’s intuitiveness and support team are strong points.

**Q: What tutors do not like about Thinkific:** On the other hand, tutors report some drawbacks. A common issue is that uploading content can be slow – one review stated “the uploading can be quite slow” even on a fast connection. Others mention that advanced customization (like coding your own design) only comes on higher plans. Customer feedback also points to troubles with billing and refund policies. Some instructors feel the support can be slow to answer complex questions. In summary, tutors wish for faster performance and more flexibility unless they upgrade to costly plans.

**Q: Does Thinkific charge any transaction fees?** No. Thinkific does not take a cut of your course sales. Instructors keep all the money students pay, except the standard credit card fees. According to Thinkific documentation, they charge 0% transaction fees on sales. You only pay the normal Stripe/PayPal fee (around 2.9% + $0.30) for each sale.

**Q: Does Thinkific have a free plan?** No, Thinkific does not offer a free forever plan. It only offers a 30-day free trial during which you can test all features. After the trial, you must pick a paid plan to continue using the platform. There is no permanent no-cost plan.

**Q: What payment methods does Thinkific support?** Thinkific accepts all major payment methods. It has its own Stripe-based processor (Thinkific Payments) and also integrates with PayPal. This means students can pay by credit card (Visa, MasterCard, etc.) or PayPal in a secure checkout. Tutors can connect their Stripe or PayPal accounts to receive payments.

**Q: What languages does Thinkific support?** Thinkific’s interface and content can be in multiple languages. The company site and admin panel support many languages (for example, English, Spanish, German, etc.). Instructors can create courses in any language and customize the course text. Students around the world (over 165 countries) use Thinkific, though the primary language for the platform is English.

**Q: Can students use Thinkific courses on mobile devices?** Yes. Thinkific courses are mobile-friendly by default. Students can log in on smartphones or tablets via a web browser. Instructors can also create a custom branded mobile app for their courses on higher plans, allowing students to access content on iOS/Android. In any case, the platform is designed to work on all screen sizes.

**Q: Is Thinkific secure and reliable?** Thinkific is considered safe. It uses SSL encryption for all data and payments, and it is publicly traded in Canada (TSX:THNC). The platform is used by well-known companies (like Hootsuite and GoDaddy), which speaks to its reliability. Thinkific also states compliance with privacy laws (GDPR, etc.). As with any online service, using strong passwords and payment security best practices is wise, but Thinkific’s own infrastructure is standard for educational platforms.

**Q: Can I cancel my Thinkific subscription any time?** Yes. You can upgrade, downgrade or cancel your plan at any time, including during the free trial. There is no minimum commitment. Simply go to your account settings to cancel, and your subscription will stop before the next billing cycle.

### Conclusion

Thinkific’s strengths lie in its powerful course-building features, user-friendly interface, and robust sales tools. It is well-suited for entrepreneurs and educators [who want to create and sell online](https://www.thinkific.com/experts/) courses at scale. Its weaknesses include higher-tier pricing, limited customization on basic plans, and reported customer support issues. For those seeking personalized tutoring or advanced STEM help, My Engineering Buddy offers one-on-one sessions and [expert tutors](https://www.myengineeringbuddy.com/tutors/), which Thinkific does not provide. In short, Thinkific is great for self-paced online courses, while My Engineering Buddy may be a [better choice for students](https://www.myengineeringbuddy.com/contact-us/) needing in-depth homework help and direct teacher support.

Thinkific is one of the most popular course platforms for educators, coaches, and entrepreneurs in 2026. With its all-in-one structure and flexible tools, Thinkific makes it easier to turn your expertise into a successful training business.

In this article, we talk about how much Thinkific costs in 2026, what features you get on each plan, whether there are hidden fees, and what other cheaper or more powerful alternatives are available.

## **Thinkific Pricing Plans in 2026**

Thinkific offers four main pricing plans:

|     |     |     |     |     |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Plan | Monthly Price | Annual Price Paid Upfront | Transaction Fees | Best For |
| Basic | $49/month | $36/month ($432/year) | 0% | Creators launching their first course |
| Start | $99/month | $74/month ($888/year) | 0% | Growing course businesses |
| Grow | $199/month | $149/month ($1,788/year) | 0% | Advanced businesses scaling operations |
| Plus | Custom pricing | Custom pricing | 0% | Large enterprises |

Let’s explore each one.

## **Thinkific Basic Monthly & Annual Plan**

The Basic Plan is Thinkific’s entry plan and is targeted at beginners to course creation. At $49/month or $36/month ($432 paid upfront) if billed annually giving savings of $156 annually compared to the monthly plan.This plan unlocks a bunch of business-friendly features without being overwhelming.

### **What’s Included in the Basic Plan:**

- Unlimited courses
- 1 community
- 5 Coaching & Webinar sessions
- 5 Digital Downloads
- Sales tool
- Abandoned cart emails

### **Who Is It Best For?**

You should consider the Basic Plan if:

- You’re launching your first paid course
- You don’t need advanced marketing and sales tools
- You’re on a budget

## **Thinkific Start Monthly & Annual Plan**

The Start Plan is where things start getting interesting. This plan costs $99/month for the monthly plan or $74/month if billed annually ($888 paid upfront), you get decent savings of $300 annually compared to the monthly plan. This plan is built for creators who want to grow their online school and optimize the student experience.

### **What’s Included:**

Everything in the Basic Plan, plus:

- Unlimited Live Lessons
- Unlimited Memberships
- Unlimited Digital Downloads
- Unlimited Coaching & Webinar sessions
- Edit HTML & CSS
- Sell Bundles and Add-ons

### **Who Should Use It?**

You should pick the Start Plan if:

- You have a successful, growing course business
- You plan to host live coaching calls or workshops
- You need more content flexibility
- You need a more customized experience

## **Thinkific Grow Monthly & Annual Plan**

If you’re running a serious online education business, the Grow Plan offers advanced tools to help you scale. Billed at $199/month for the monthly plan or $149/month for the annual plan which gives impressive savings of $600, it gives you deeper analytics, expanded admin access, and priority support.

### **What You Get:**

All Start Plan features, plus:

- 3 communities
- Remove Thinkific branding
- Thinkific Analytics
- Group Orders
- Invoicing
- Gifting
- API Access

### **Who Is It For?**

Go with Grow if:

- You have multiple courses
- You want to build engaged community of learners
- You manage a team and need multi-admin access
- You're scaling up course sales and need advanced data
- You want priority help and more backend flexibility

## **Thinkific Plus Monthly & Annual Plan**

This plan is designed for high-growth businesses. It unlocks enterprise-level features such as advanced integrations, and tailored support to scale your online learning business.

## **Thinkific Pricing Plan Comparison**

|     |     |     |     |     |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Feature | Basic | Start | Grow | Plus |
| Monthly Price | $49 | $99 | $199 | Custom pricing |
| Annual Price | $432 ($36/mo) | $888 ($74/mo) | $1,788 ($149/mo) | Custom pricing |
| Courses | Unlimited | Unlimited | Unlimited | Unlimited |
| Communities | ❌ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Live Lessons | ❌ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Affiliate Reporting | ❌ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Priority Support | ❌ | ❌ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Custom Domain | ❌ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |

## **Are There Any Hidden Fees?**

Unlike other platforms, Thinkific has no transaction fees on any of its plans. However, you’ll still pay standard payment processing fees through Stripe or PayPal. These are typically:

- Stripe: 2.9% + 30¢ per transaction
- PayPal: Around 3.49% + a fixed fee depending on region

These fees are industry standard and aren’t unique to Thinkific.

You might need to spend some extra money paying for advanced email marketing and sales tools since Thinkifc has only basic tools but this is optional.

## **Which Thinkific Plan Should You Choose?**

Here’s a simple way to decide:

- Launching your first course? Go with the Basic Plan.
- Growing your business and want live lessons, communities, and more? Choose the Start Plan.
- Scaling with a team and need powerful analytics and dev options? Go with the Grow plan.
- In need of enterprise level features and customization? Talk to sales for the Plus plan.

## **Thinkific Free Plan**

Thinkific used to have a free plan but as of 2026, it no longer exists. The old free plan allowed users to create1 course and provided full access to Thinkific’s course builder. Thinkific now offers a 14 day free trial that gives you a feel of what the platform offers.

## **Cheaper Alternatives to Thinkific in 2026**

Thinkific is a solid product, but not necessarily the cheapest. If you're tight on budget or need something simpler or more niche, here are some alternatives:

### **1\. SchoolMaker**: $29/month starting price

- Zero transaction fee on all plans
- Sell courses and coaching
- Strong course builder and community features

**Why it’s great:** You get unique features to help you sell more courses.

### **3\. Podia:** Starts at $33/month

- Sell multiple digital products
- Built-in email marketing

**Why it’s good:** Offers better email tools and a website builder than Thinkific at a lower price point.

### **4\. Teachable:** Starts at $29/month

- Popular course platform like Thinkific
- Free plan available (with $1 + 10% fee)

**Why it’s comparable:** Similar functionality to Thinkific with slightly more focus on ease of use and mobile-friendly experiences.

## **Is Thinkific’s Pricing Worth It?**

Yes it is, but there are cheaper alternatives if you’re price sensitive.Thinkific delivers a solid experience, is beginner-friendly, doesn’t charge transaction fees, and scales well as your business grows.

## **Conclusion**

Thinkific continues to be one of the top choices for course creators in 2026, offering transparent pricing, no hidden fees, and a professional toolset to help you grow.

Whether you're launching your first course or managing a full-blown online academy, there's likely a Thinkific plan that fits your goal, and if not, there are plenty of cheaper alternatives to consider.
